Taylor Swift: Released in 2006, this was Taylor’s first studio album, written primarily during her freshman year of high school. A fun fact: Taylor is the first female country artist to write or co-write every song on a platinum-certified debut album.
Fearless: This second studio album was released in 2008 and explored the fearless nature of teenage love and heartbreak. It famously became the most awarded country album of all time and won Taylor her first Grammy for Album of the Year.
Speak Now: Written entirely by Taylor herself between the ages of 18 and 20, her third album was released in 2010 to prove she didn't need co-writers to be successful. One of the vinyl variants was so sought after that a temporary worker in France was actually sentenced to prison for stealing copies from a warehouse before release.
Red: Released in 2012, Taylor’s fourth album serves as her primary breakup record, transitioning from country to pop. The original version of the fan-favorite track All Too Well was initially ten minutes long, a version she eventually released nearly a decade later.
1989: This fifth studio album, released in 2014, marked her official shift into 'shimmery' synth-pop and was named after her birth year. It made her the first woman to win Album of the Year at the Grammys twice.
My favourite track: Shake It Off
Reputation: Her sixth album was released in 2017 after a year-long hiatus from the public eye and addressed the 'reputation' built for her by the media. Despite the dark, edgy marketing, Taylor has described the record as a love story at its core.
Lover: Released in 2019, this seventh album was the first record Taylor fully owned herself. It was originally going to be titled Daylight, but she changed it after writing the title track.
Folklore: Taylor’s eighth album was a surprise release in the summer of 2020, written and recorded in total secrecy during the COVID-19 pandemic. It saw her move into an indie-folk sound and use fictional storytelling rather than strictly autobiographical lyrics.
My favourite track: My Tears Ricochet
Evermore: The ninth studio album, released in late 2020, was described as Folklore's sister record and was announced just two days before Taylor's 31st birthday. She completed the track 'happiness' only six days before the album was mastered.
Midnights: Released in 2022, this tenth album is a concept record based on '13 sleepless nights' scattered throughout Taylor's life. It became the first album in history to monopolize the entire Top 10 of the Billboard Hot 100 in a single week.
The Tortured Poets Department: Released in April 2024, this eleventh album was a 'lifeline' for Taylor, written during the height of her Eras Tour. Two hours after the standard release, she surprised fans by revealing it was actually a double album titled The Anthology.
My favourite track: I Can Do It With A Broken Heart
The Life of a Showgirl: Her twelfth studio album was released on October 3, 2025, and marks a return to collaborations with producers Max Martin and Shellback. It features a notable collaboration with Sabrina Carpenter on the title track.
